description: CRM tracking for COMARE gym maintenance outreach in Mexico City

icp.ideal_customer: Mid-size gym chains in Mexico City
characteristics: Franchise model (Smart Fit, Body Tech, Kraken)
pain_points: High member churn from equipment failures

prospects.name: Smart Fit Mexico
prospects.title: Operations
prospects.notes: 80-100 new locations = massive opportunity. Low-cost model = cost efficiency critical. COMARE's fixed monthly fee = perfect fit.

prospects.name: Body Tech
prospects.title: Facilities
prospects.notes: High-end gym model = premium maintenance expectations. Good fit for COMARE.

prospects.name: Kraken Fitness
prospects.title: Operations
prospects.notes: Expansion mode = new maintenance setup needed. Perfect timing.

email_template.subject: Quick question about {company}'s equipment maintenance
email_template.body:
Hi {name},

I'm Jordan — working with a gym maintenance company in Mexico City.

Most gyms we work with spend $3,000-5,000/month on reactive maintenance. Equipment breaks, they scramble to fix it.

The cost isn't just the repair:
- Member complaints when equipment doesn't work
- Cancellations when it happens repeatedly
- Staff time managing the chaos

We handle all maintenance for a fixed monthly fee. Members never see a broken machine.

Would a 15-minute call this week make sense?

Best,
Jordan
+34 612 22 38 79

money_map.true_cost_breakdown: Emergency calls: $500/month avg, Equipment degradation: $3,000/year, Member churn: $5,000/year
money_map.comare_solution: Fixed monthly fee = predictable, Preventive maintenance prevents emergencies
